title: [epic] Extract Renode STM32WL SubGHz peripheral as standalone project
description:
Extract LichenSubGHz.cs and STM32WL support as standalone Renode package.

WHY THIS DOESNT EXIST:
- STM32WL is newer (2020), Renode support lagging
- SubGHz radio is internal to chip, unusual architecture
- ST provides CubeWL but no simulation
- LoRaWAN certification done on hardware
- Seeed Lora-E5 users are makers, not CI-focused

WHAT WE HAVE:
- LichenSubGHz.cs (341 lines) - STM32WL integrated SubGHz radio
- STM32WL_PWR.cs (99 lines) - power controller stub
- STM32WL_RCC.cs (117 lines) - clock controller stub
- Nucleo-WL55JC .repl platform definition

EXTRACTION SCOPE:
1. Standalone repo: renode-stm32wl-subghz
2. SubGHz peripheral with TX/RX support
3. Power and clock stubs for boot
4. Nucleo-WL55JC board definition
5. Example: Zephyr LoRa sample boots and transmits

TARGET USERS: STM32WL devs, Seeed Lora-E5 users, LoRaWAN stack devs

ACCEPTANCE: Zephyr lora_send sample runs in Renode on Nucleo-WL55JC
